name: Apple-Almond Deep-Dish Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cold Unsalted Butter, cut into cubes
3 tablespoons Unsalted Butter, cut into bits
recipeInstructions:
1. ALMOND CRUST: In a mixing bowl or food processor, combine the flour, sugar, salt, finely ground almonds, cold butter, and shortening until crumbled to pea-sized pieces. Mix almond extract with 2 tablespoons of milk; stir into the crumbled mixture. Add enough additional cold milk to form a dough. Form the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic, and chill for at least 1 hour.
2. FILLING: In a mixing bowl, combine apple slices, sugar, and butter. Transfer the mixture to a deep 9 1/2-inch pie dish, mounding apples in the center.
3. Preheat oven to 425°F. Roll out almond dough on a lightly floured surface into a neat 12-inch round. Fold dough in half and gently transfer it to the pie dish, unfolding over apples. Fold overhanging pastry edges toward the dough's surface, forming a thick border around the edges. Flute edges and cut several slashes in the center of the crust.
4. Bake the pie for 15 minutes; then reduce temperature to 350°F and continue baking until pastry is golden and apples are tender when pierced with a fork, about 35-40 minutes longer.
5. Remove from oven. Using a funnel, pour cider, applejack, or brandy through one of the steam vents in the pastry. Cool on wire rack; serve warm or at room temperature in deep bowls, passing cream separately if desired.
